2606.26205.title: Knowledge-augmented Agentic AI for Mental Health Medication Information Seeking
2606.26205.paper_summary: Patients increasingly seek medication information online, yet safety knowledge for psychiatric drugs is split between regulatory adverse-event records, which are authoritative but abstract, and patient narratives, which are experience-near but unvalidated. Integrating them without conflating evidence and anecdote is especially consequential in psychiatry, where poorly contextualised information can amplify fear, nocebo responses, and non-adherence. Here we develop a provenance-aware, knowledge-graph-based multi-agent framework unifying 466,525 Reddit posts, 60,782 WebMD reviews, and twenty years of U.S. FDA Adverse Event Reporting System records for nine antidepressants. A large-language-model entity-recognition pipeline benchmarked against physician annotations reached highest F1 scores of 0.969 for medications and 0.973 for conditions. The two community platforms were far more concordant with each other (overlap up to a Jaccard similarity of 0.905) than with regulatory reports, indicating that patient-generated data form a partly independent safety signal. For sertraline, many adverse events appeared in community sources hundreds of days before the corresponding FDA date. A Neo4j knowledge graph grounded in ATC-N, ICD-10, and MedDRA vocabularies preserves provenance, keeping every claim traceable and regulatory facts distinct from patient experience. These results establish source-aware integration as a route to more auditable psychiatric medication information, with usefulness and patient benefit to be tested prospectively.
2606.26205.paper_summary_zh: 患者越來越多地在網上尋求藥物資訊，但精神科藥物的安全知識卻分為兩部分：一方面是權威但抽象的監管不良事件記錄，另一方面是接近經驗但未經驗證的患者敘述。在精神科中，將這兩者整合而不混淆證據與轶事尤其重要，因為缺乏情境的資訊可能會加劇恐懼、負面反應和不遵從治療。在這裡，我們開發了一個基於知識圖譜的多代理框架，整合了466,525條Reddit帖子、60,782條WebMD評論，以及美國FDA不良事件報告系統的二十年記錄，針對九種抗抑鬱藥物。一個基於大型語言模型的實體識別管道，與醫生標註進行基準測試，對藥物和病症的F1分數分別達到0.969和0.973。這兩個社區平台之間的相符程度遠高於與監管報告的相符程度（重疊達到0.905的Jaccard相似度），這表明患者生成的數據形成了部分獨立的安全信號。對於舍曲林，許多不良事件在社區來源中出現的時間早於相應的FDA日期數百天。一個基於ATC-N、ICD-10和MedDRA詞彙的Neo4j知識圖譜保留了來源，保持每個主張可追溯，並將監管事實與患者經驗區分開來。這些結果確立了源感知整合作為獲得更可審計的精神科藥物資訊的途徑，其實用性和患者利益將在前瞻性研究中進行測試。
2606.26205.author: Huizi Yu et.al.
2606.26205.authors: Huizi Yu, Jian Liu, Wenkong Wang, Lingyao Li, Jiayan Zhou, Zhaoqian Xue, Xiang Li, Xinxin Lin, Zhiying Liang, Zhuoru Wu, Siyuan Ma, Xin Ma, Lizhou Fan
